growth_records.medium: CDMG (chemically defined medium CDM + 2% glucose), agar plate

up.method: HPLC / chromogenic depletion; derived volumetric average

growth_records.cite: Hui Liao et al. Slower-growing species promote interspecific cooperation and coexistence under acid stress through cross-feeding. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-67395-z. [PMC12816087]
growth_records.snippet: In LM pure culture, glucose was depleted at 27 h (Fig. S21A, Orange square) and lactate reached a maximum of 121.7 mM (Fig. S21A, Red circle)
growth_records.notes: Glucose 2% (w/v) assumed = 20 g/L; MW 180.16 -> 20/180.16 = 111.0 mM. Depleted by 27 h -> uptake = 111.0/27 = 4.11 mmol/L/h. Lactate 121.7 mM at 27 h -> secretion = 121.7/27 = 4.51 mmol/L/h. Lactate/glucose yield = 121.7/111.0 = 1.10 mol/mol (consistent with heterofermentative Leuconostoc: ~1 lactate + 1 ethanol + 1 CO2 per glucose). Rates are VOLUMETRIC batch averages (mM/h); biomass reported only as OD600 in figures, no gDW available so cannot convert to mmol/gDW/h. This is a spatially-structured agar culture, so rates are apparent/lumped.

growth_records.medium: CDMG (chemically defined medium CDM + 2% glucose), well-mixed liquid
growth_records.cite: Hui Liao et al. Slower-growing species promote interspecific cooperation and coexistence under acid stress through cross-feeding. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-67395-z. [PMC12816087]
growth_records.snippet: ...lactate reached a maximum of 121.7 mM..., significantly lower than 193.4 mM ... in well-mixed liquid culture (Fig. S21B), indicating that the lactate released during rapid growth on glucose diffuses directly into the medium
growth_records.notes: Well-mixed liquid LM monoculture reached max lactate 193.4 mM (vs 121.7 mM on agar). No time-course value stated in text for the liquid endpoint, so NO rate derivable. Yield 193.4/111.0 = 1.74 mol lactate/mol glucose exceeds 1.0, implying additional carbon/substrate in the base CDM (amino acids/citrate) or measurement scope; flagged. Distinct condition from the agar record (well-mixed vs spatially structured).

up.method: enzymatic kit (R-Biopharm) on culture supernatant; not normalised to biomass/time

growth_records.cite: Ricciardi A et al. The Effect of Respiration, pH, and Citrate Co-Metabolism on the Growth, Metabolite Production and Enzymatic Activities of Leuconostoc mesenteroides subsp. cremoris E30. Foods (2022). DOI:10.3390/foods11040535. [PMC8871477]
growth_records.snippet: F1_AN_0_6.5 ... umax 0.46 h-1; stationary: biomass yield 0.072, glucose consumed 131.93 mM, lactate yield 0.979, acetate yield 0.019, ethanol yield 0.682 g/g
growth_records.notes: Heterofermentative LAB, phosphoketolase pathway. umax = mean of two biological replicates (0.46, 0.46 h-1) from Table 2. doubling_time td = ln2/umax = 0.693/0.46 = 1.51 h (calculated). Glucose consumed reported as mM (S0-S), Table 3; not convertible to mmol/gDW/h without biomass-time course. Yields are g product/g glucose consumed (Table 3), reported directly. Note Table 3 footnote mislabels ethanol yield formula as '(E-E0)' relative to consumed glucose. Full mWMB base composition not given in text (cited refs [25],[26]); only glucose 20 g/L stated.
